compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DERISK (E5): replication identity = a single-repo WRITE deploy key. navapbc/rebar currently has 0 deploy keys; S5 adds the key (private half in SSM) and S6 bypass-lists it. Replication plugin authenticates via passwordless SSH public key (per docs) â€” matches the deploy-key approach.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S5 implemented + verified LIVE â€” 2026-06-30 (commit dd36a3750)

Replication plugin (bundled) configured + deploy key live. navabpc/rebar main UNCHANGED at cea18e2 throughout (no real-main advance â€” that is S7's e2e).

**AC verification (subagent + alarm test, all PASS):**
- AC1 â€” Replication push works: replication_log shows `Push to ...rebar.git ... refs/heads/main:refs/heads/main force=no ... 0 retries`; a tag pushed to Gerrit replicated to GitHub (`git ls-remote` showed gerrit-repl-smoke=cea18e2), then deleted from both (cleanup confirmed).
- AC2 â€” projects=rebar binding: only `rebar` replicates (config + log show only `rebar:` scheduling).
- AC3 â€” refs/changes/* + refs/meta/config NOT pushed: `git ls-remote github | grep refs/changes|refs/meta` empty.
- AC4 â€” mirror=false, replicationMaxRetries=3, timeout=60.
- AC5 â€” `[replication] eventsDirectory=data/replication`; /var/gerrit/data/replication exists (crash-safe queue).
- AC6 â€” NON-FORCE enforced: forced Gerrit main to a divergent root; replication returned `REJECTED_NONFASTFORWARD`; **GitHub main stayed cea18e2 (unchanged)**. Gerrit main restored.
- AC7 â€” deploy key on disk: /var/gerrit/.ssh/id_ed25519 mode 0600 owner uid 1000; ~/.ssh/config routes github.com to it.
- AC8 â€” fail-closed: materialize-deploy-key.sh with a bogus SSM param exits non-zero (ParameterNotFound), writes no key.
- AC9 â€” `ssh -T git@github.com` (as gerrit user) -> "Hi navapbc/rebar! You've successfully authenticated".
- AC10 â€” reproducible as-code: register-deploy-key.sh + materialize-deploy-key.sh + setup-replication.sh + replication.config (idempotent).
- AC11 â€” replication-failure ALERT fires: CloudWatch alarm `rebar-gerrit-replication-errors` on `rebar/host:replication_errors`; the observability probe detected the AC6 non-FF line and published the metric -> alarm transitioned OK -> ALARM (verified); recovered to OK after a 0 datapoint.
- AC12 â€” kill-switch <5min: `mv replication.config aside` + restart -> "not replicating"; restored -> "Succeeded! (OK)".
- ADR-0010 committed (one-way-door contract, BSL note, rotation, kill-switch).

**Deploy key:** registered on navapbc/rebar (write-enabled, id 155914477), private key from SSM /rebar/prod/github-replication-deploy-key.
**NOTE for S7:** admin cannot direct-push to refs/heads/main (review-gated, by design) â€” the real-main advance e2e must go through the review/submit flow (or a deliberate temp ACL).

**Code review (subagent):** VERDICT was CHANGES-REQUESTED for 1 blocker â€” observability.sh `grep -c || echo 0` corrupted the count on the healthy zero-failure path. FIXED (drop `|| echo 0`, `total=${total:-0}`); redeployed + verified clean (total=1 single line, new=0, publishes 0). NITs (ssh-keyscan TOFU; dimension-contract comment) addressed/noted.

compiled_state.description:
# S5 â€” Gerrit -> GitHub one-way replication (core plugin, non-force, deploy key)

## Why
Make Gerrit the sole writer of GitHub `main`. The core, Apache-2.0, WAR-bundled `replication`
plugin (push) is the right tool â€” the GerritForge BSL re-license (2025-09-30) touched only
`pull-replication`/multi-site, not this plugin. The non-force / `mirror=false` / deploy-key
choices form a one-way-door replication contract (Gerrit is the only writer; GitHub history is
append-only/fast-forward), captured as an ADR (## Files).

## What
- `replication.config` (`infra/gerrit/replication.config`):
  - `url = git@github.com:navapbc/rebar.git` (scp-style SSH).
  - **`projects = rebar`** (peer-pattern correction â€” REQUIRED). Because the URL
    hardcodes the single repo and uses no `${name}` interpolation, WITHOUT a `projects =`
    filter the remote matches **every** Gerrit project and tries to push each one's `main`+tags
    into the one GitHub repo. Bind it to exactly the `rebar` project.
  - `push = refs/heads/main:refs/heads/main` + `push = refs/tags/*:refs/tags/*` (NON-force â€” no
    leading `+`).
  - `mirror=false` (true does a destructive prune of remote refs absent/invisible locally).
  - `replicatePermissions=false` (don't leak `refs/meta/config`); explicit refspecs so
    `refs/changes/*` is never pushed.
  - finite `replicationMaxRetries = 3`; `timeout = 60` (seconds). Values are concrete; they may
    be tuned but must remain finite/bounded â€” unlimited retries are forbidden (see AC).
- **Crash-safe queue (peer-pattern correction).** Set `[replication] eventsDirectory =
  data/replication` in the plugin's own `replication.config` (NOT `gerrit.config`, where the
  replication plugin silently ignores it) so pending replication
  events survive a Gerrit restart. Without it, the push queue is in-memory and a restart
  mid-queue silently drops pending pushes, leaving the GitHub mirror stale.
- Identity: a single-repo, write-enabled, passphrase-less GitHub deploy key on `navapbc/rebar`
  (currently **0 deploy keys** â€” S5 adds exactly one; GitHub-side bypass-listing is S6's job).
  Private key from **SSM Parameter Store** at `/rebar/prod/github-deploy-key` (SecureString).
  The replication plugin's SSH client runs as the **Gerrit OS user** (`gerrit2`), so the key +
  SSH config live in `/home/gerrit2/.ssh/`, not root's.
- Provisioning is **as-code and idempotent**: the GitHub deploy-key registration + SSM parameter
  are managed by IaC (Terraform `github_repository_deploy_key` + `aws_ssm_parameter`, or an
  equivalent checked-in idempotent script), and an init/entrypoint hook materialises the key from
  SSM + seeds `known_hosts` **before** the replication plugin can fire its first push.
- **SSM->disk materialisation + ordering (the most common failure mode):** an entrypoint step,
  run as/writing to `gerrit2` and ordered **before** Gerrit's main process starts, must: (1)
  `aws ssm get-parameter --name /rebar/prod/github-deploy-key --with-decryption` -> write
  `/home/gerrit2/.ssh/id_ed25519` owned by `gerrit2`, mode `0600`; (2) `ssh-keyscan github.com
  >> /home/gerrit2/.ssh/known_hosts` (non-interactive first connection); (3) write `~/.ssh/config`
  `Host github.com` with `IdentityFile` pointing at that key. This step **MUST hard-fail
  (fail-closed):** on ANY `aws ssm get-parameter` failure (throttle, IAM-propagation lag,
  network-not-ready) or a missing/empty key value it **exits non-zero and ABORTS Gerrit startup**
  â€” never continue and let Gerrit start without the deploy key, since that yields silent
  replication failure rather than a loud one.
- **Lifecycle / rotation (one-way-door â€” in the ADR):** rotation = generate new keypair -> add
  the new GitHub deploy key -> update SSM -> reload the replication plugin -> remove the old key
  (no outage gap). Reproducible from checked-in IaC.
- **Observability:** a persistent alert (not just the one-time check) on `replication_log`
  ERROR / max-retry-exhaustion / `REJECTED_NONFASTFORWARD` â€” a log-based metric + alarm so
  operators are notified when replication stalls (revoked key, network partition, non-FF
  rejection) instead of GitHub silently going stale.
- **Rollback / kill-switch:** replication disabled in <5 min without a full redeploy by renaming
  the config + reloading the plugin (`mv replication.config replication.config.disabled && ssh
  -p 29418 <gerrit> gerrit plugin reload replication`); if the key is suspected compromised,
  revoke the GitHub deploy key + rotate SSM.

## Scope
In: replication plugin config (incl. `projects=` + crash-safe `eventsDirectory`) + the
deploy-key identity (SSM->disk, `~/.ssh/config`, `known_hosts`) + as-code provisioning + push of
`main`+tags + replication-failure alerting + a documented rollback/kill-switch + the ADR. Out:
the GitHub-side lockdown that makes the mirror read-only (S6). **S5->S6 ordering (do not
ignore):** S5 turns on non-force push **before** S6 forbids direct GitHub writes, so a direct
push to GitHub `main` in that window would diverge it and cause every subsequent non-force
replication to be rejected `non-fast-forward`. Mitigate by (a) deploying S6 in the same
change-set or promptly after, AND (b) the pre-activation prerequisite + non-FF alert below.

### Pre-activation prerequisite
Before enabling replication, verify GitHub `main` is fast-forwardable from Gerrit's HEAD:
`git ls-remote https://github.com/navapbc/rebar refs/heads/main` HEAD must be an ancestor of (or
equal to) Gerrit's `main` HEAD. If GitHub is already ahead, resolve the divergence first.

## Files
- `infra/gerrit/replication.config` â€” push refspecs, `projects=`, `mirror=false`,
  `replicatePermissions=false`, finite retries, timeout.
- `infra/gerrit/replication.config` â€” holds the ENTIRE replication config: `[remote "github"]` (url, non-force push refspecs, `replicatePermissions=false`), `projects=rebar`, AND `[replication] eventsDirectory = data/replication`. (NOT `gerrit.config` â€” the plugin reads only its own `replication.config`.)
- `infra/gerrit/entrypoint.sh` (or init hook) â€” SSM->disk key materialisation + `ssh-keyscan`
  known_hosts + `~/.ssh/config`, run as `gerrit2` before Gerrit starts.
- `infra/` deploy-key provisioning as-code â€” Terraform `github_repository_deploy_key` +
  `aws_ssm_parameter` (SecureString at `/rebar/prod/github-deploy-key`) or a checked-in
  idempotent script; includes teardown.
- `infra/` replication-failure alert definition (log-based metric + alarm on `replication_log`
  ERROR / max-retry / `REJECTED_NONFASTFORWARD`).
- `docs/adr/0010-gerrit-github-replication.md` â€” ADR: plugin choice + BSL rationale, non-force /
  fast-forward-only policy, `mirror=false`, `projects=` single-repo binding, crash-safe
  `eventsDirectory`, deploy-key rotation lifecycle, and the pointer to S6.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A change submitted in Gerrit replicates `main` to `navapbc/rebar` (non-force, fast-forward)
      and tags replicate, verified by `replication_log` exit-0 + `git ls-remote` reflecting the SHA.
- [ ] The remote is bound via `projects = rebar` so ONLY the rebar project replicates
      to the GitHub repo (no other Gerrit project is pushed there), verified by config + log.
- [ ] `refs/changes/*` and `refs/meta/config` are NOT pushed to GitHub (explicit refspecs +
      `replicatePermissions=false`), verified by inspecting GitHub refs after replication.
- [ ] `mirror=false` (no destructive pruning) and `replicationMaxRetries` is finite (e.g. 3,
      never unlimited) with a bounded `timeout`.
- [ ] `[replication] eventsDirectory` is set so the replication queue is crash-safe across a
      Gerrit restart (a restart mid-queue does not silently drop pending pushes).
- [ ] **Non-force is enforced end-to-end:** given a GitHub `main` that cannot be fast-forwarded,
      the push is **rejected** â€” `replication_log` records `non-fast-forward` (exit non-zero) and
      GitHub history is **unchanged** (guards against a stray `+`).
- [ ] The replication identity is a single-repo write deploy key whose private key is from SSM
      (no plaintext in VCS); on the host `/home/gerrit2/.ssh/id_ed25519` exists owned by
      `gerrit2` mode `0600`, `~/.ssh/config` routes `github.com` to it, `known_hosts` pre-seeded.
- [ ] **The entrypoint SSM-materialisation step hard-fails (fail-closed):** simulating an `aws
      ssm get-parameter` failure (or a missing/empty key) causes the step to exit non-zero and
      Gerrit **does not start** â€” Gerrit never comes up without the replication deploy key.
- [ ] A pre-activation SSH smoke-test passes before go-live: `sudo -u gerrit2 ssh -T
      git@github.com` returns GitHub's "successfully authenticated" banner (no host-key prompt).
- [ ] Deploy-key + SSM provisioning is reproducible as-code (re-run on a clean host recreates
      key + SSM param + on-disk key + known_hosts, no manual steps); teardown documented.
- [ ] A persistent replication-failure alert fires (in a test) on an ERROR / max-retry /
      `non-fast-forward` entry in `replication_log`, so a silent stall in the S5->S6 window is caught.
- [ ] A rollback/kill-switch works: replication disabled in <5 min via `mv replication.config
      replication.config.disabled` + `gerrit plugin reload replication`, with deploy-key
      revocation as the compromise path.
- [ ] `docs/adr/0010-gerrit-github-replication.md` captures the replication-contract decisions.

## Round-7 remediation
- **Concrete project name (E2):** `projects = rebar` (the project S3 creates on the Gerrit instance) â€” not a placeholder.

## Round-8 remediation
- **eventsDirectory location (G6):** `[replication] eventsDirectory = data/replication` belongs in `replication.config` (the replication plugin's OWN config file), NOT `gerrit.config` (Gerrit silently ignores the unknown stanza there). Place it in `infra/gerrit/replication.config`.

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- BSL scope RESOLVED (Round-12): GerritForge's 2025-09-30 re-license (gitenterprise.me/2025/09/30/re-licensing-gerritforge-plugins-welcome-to-gerrit-enterprise) moved the **pull-replication** and **multi-site** GerritForge plugins to BSL (their Apache-2.0 versions deprecated). The **core `replication` (push) plugin used here ships with Gerrit Core under Apache-2.0** â€” GerritForge explicitly keeps the widely-used/core plugins Apache-2.0. This epic uses push `replication`, NOT pull-replication or multi-site, so it is unaffected. No legal review needed at build; ADR-0010 records this citation + a 1-line LICENSE-string check of the bundled `replication` jar in the pinned 3.14.1 image as the mechanical confirmation.

Added ACs:
- [ ] ADR-0010 cites the BSL re-license scope (or records the LICENSE-in-image verification) confirming the `replication` push plugin used here is Apache-2.0, not BSL-restricted

file_impact.reason: replication plugin remote github: url, refspecs, replicatePermissions=false, projects=rebar, eventsDirectory
install replication.config + register deploy key + known_hosts; trigger replication
ADR replication design + BSL license note

compiled_state.title: infra: S5 â€” Gerrit->GitHub one-way replication (core plugin, non-force, deploy key)
